---
title: "AEM 7010: Doing Applied Economics Research: Practical Skills"
subtitle: "Spring 2026 — Prof. Ariel Ortiz-Bobea"
---

## Overview

This course accelerates your learning curve on skills that are rarely taught but critical for research productivity. Topics include data management, reproducibility, version control, and the use of AI tools in research.

**Schedule:** April 8 – May 4, 2026 | Mon/Wed, 8:40–9:55 AM | Warren 137

## Sessions

| # | Date | Topic | Materials |
|---|------|-------|-----------|
| 1 | Apr 8 | [Introduction](1-intro/index.qmd) | Slides |
| 2 | Apr 13 | [Reproducibility I](2-3-reproducibility/index.qmd) | Guest: Lars Vilhuber |
| 3 | Apr 15 | [Reproducibility II](2-3-reproducibility/index.qmd) | Guest: Lars Vilhuber |
| 4 | Apr 20 | [Git Fundamentals](4-5-git/index.qmd) | Slides + Walkthrough |
| 5 | Apr 22 | [GitHub & Collaboration](4-5-git/index.qmd) | Slides + Walkthrough |
| 6 | Apr 27 | [AI Tools for Research I](6-7-ai/index.qmd) | Slides + Walkthrough |
| 7 | Apr 29 | [AI Tools for Research II](6-7-ai/index.qmd) | Slides + Walkthrough |
| 8 | May 4 | [Cornell Resources](8-resources/index.qmd) | Slides |

## Key principles

- You are not writing code for yourself, but for others — including yourself at *t+1*.
- Invest in workflow skills early. The returns compound over your career.
- Any version control system is better than none.
- AI tools are powerful but require judgment and verification.

## Instructor

Prof. Ariel Ortiz-Bobea, Dyson and Brooks Schools, Cornell University.
[Course Canvas site](https://canvas.cornell.edu/courses/85144) | Office hours: Wednesdays 2–4 PM, Warren 450B
